posts.raw_content:
EDIT: 4:30 EST: I have to step away for a while. Your thoughts and prayers are overwhelming and I haven't stopped crying the entire time. Even dad with his dementia is starting to notice so I need to take a break. Thank you all so very much. I can honestly feel warmth of your compassion for mom, dad and myself.

Original post:
I always read these types of posts but never thought I would be making one. I know this forum isn't meant for this type of post but I'm so desperate for prayers to help mom in her time of need. Mom is 90 and broke her hip end of January (spent her 90th in hospital). The physio was going well until the hip replacement failed in February and they had to perform another hip replacement. Ever since mom has been suffering delirium. Before all of this mom and dad lived in their home and mom still took care of things. She cooked, did laundry, took care of dad (he has dementia) and cleaning was her meditation. Just a few weeks earlier she was scrubbing the bathroom floor. Amazing stuff for an 89 year old at that time. I would stop by every day to check in on them, watch funny pet videos, do groceries, yard work etc. Mom has been in hospital for 10 weeks now and has aged so much. Mom was getting depressed lately, wanting to be home but that wasn't possible yet. While in hospital mom hasn't been eating well, complaining the food was horrible. She still managed to eat a little but has now almost completely stopped eating and only drinking some protein drinks. I blame the depression for this. For the first time today while visiting, mom barely woke up to talk with me when she's normally attentive. I can only manage to visit mom an hour every day while a personal support worker is with dad because he can't be left alone. I moved in 10 weeks ago when mom was admitted to hospital to take care of dad. The dad I knew was lost three years ago because of dementia and because of that mom and I have been closer than ever. I am absolutely devastated. You don't know mom or me but you need to know she loves Trump. She has a Trump coffee mug and would only watch Fox News because she loved hearing about the things he was doing and would get upset whenever Fox didn't agree with something Trump had done. 

Mom wanted to stay home as long as possible and I did everything I could to make that happen. I realize now I let it go on too long but just loved seeing mom so happy. I also enjoyed the idea of mom and dad being a dog walk away. Things were clearly getting more difficult for mom and she realized it was time to find a care giving home. We were going to work on that when all this happened. I understand 90 is a good life but I don't want it to end like this.

I'm not able to have a happy Easter this year but I hope all of you can.

Thank you for reading this and any prayers you can say for mom. 

p.s. They just put mom on an appetite stimulant. They said when it works it it works really well. We'll find out in a couple of days. I pray it works. The lack of eating is the main problem which has also halted her physio.
